description: Strengthen a weak E2E assertion that could mask unexpected failures, and add missing E2E coverage for documented onNavigate lifecycle hook triggers (hashchange and replaceState).

userStories.title: Strengthen negative-only assertion in npm-discovery.e2e.ts to include positive verification
userStories.description: As a platform contributor, I want the npm plugin dispatch test to positively verify the expected error type (closed/unavailable tab) so that the test fails if the tool errors for an unexpected reason instead of silently passing.
acceptanceCriteria: The assertion at line ~232 of npm-discovery.e2e.ts includes both a negative check (not 'Unknown tool') and a positive check that the error content matches the expected pattern (closed tab or unavailable)
The positive assertion uses a regex or toContain check for the expected error indicators (e.g., 'closed', 'unavailable', 'no matching tab', or 'not ready')
The test still verifies that isError is true (existing assertion preserved)

userStories.title: Add onNavigate E2E tests for hashchange and replaceState triggers
userStories.description: As a plugin developer, I want E2E tests to verify that the onNavigate lifecycle hook fires on hashchange and replaceState events (not just pushState) so that regressions in these documented triggers are caught automatically.
acceptanceCriteria: A new test in lifecycle-hooks.e2e.ts verifies that onNavigate fires when window.location.hash is changed (hashchange event)
A new test in lifecycle-hooks.e2e.ts verifies that onNavigate fires when history.replaceState is called
Both tests verify the recorded URL in the __opentabs_onNavigate_urls global array contains the expected path/hash

The tests follow the same pattern as the existing 'onNavigate fires on pushState URL change' test (setupToolTest, page.evaluate to trigger navigation, waitFor to poll for the hook recording, expect assertion on the URL array)
